## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: Before using any chrome browser tools, you MUST first load them using ToolSearch.**

Chrome browser tools are MCP tools that require loading before use. Before calling any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tool:
1. Use ToolSearch with `select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__<tool_name>` to load the specific tool
2. Then call the tool

For example, to get tab context:
1. First: ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp"
2. Then: Call mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- less-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

<%# Subscribe to user's personal dashboard stream %> <%= turbo_stream_from [Current.user, "dashboard"] %>

My Sandcastles

Manage your development environments

<%= link_to new_sandbox_path, class: "inline-flex items-center gap-2 px-6 py-3 bg-blue-600 text-white font-medium rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 focus:ring-4 focus:ring-blue-200 transition-colors" do %> Create Sandcastle <% end %>
<%# Active sandboxes. The empty state is a sibling, hidden via CSS when #sandboxes has at least one child so Turbo Stream removes reveal it without a page reload. %>
<% @sandboxes.each do |sandbox| %> <%= render "sandbox", sandbox: sandbox, vnc_active: @vnc_active_ids.include?(sandbox.id), tailscale_ip: @tailscale_ips[sandbox.id] %> <% end %>

<%# Archived section is always in the DOM so Turbo Stream append/remove can update it live. Hidden via CSS when empty. Count is a child span so it can be re-rendered independently. %> <% archived_sandboxes = @archived_sandboxes.to_a %>
Archived — will be purged after retention window expires
<%= button_to "Remove all archived", purge_all_sandboxes_path, method: :delete, class: "px-3 py-1.5 text-xs bg-red-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-red-700 transition-colors", data: { action: "click->inline-confirm#confirm", confirm_message: "Permanently delete ALL archived sandcastles? This cannot be undone." } %>
<% archived_sandboxes.each do |sandbox| %> <%= render "dashboard/archived_sandbox", sandbox: sandbox %> <% end %>
<%# Show the empty state only when #sandboxes has no children, and hide the archived section when its inner list is empty. :has() is supported in every evergreen browser (Chrome 105+, Safari 15.4+, Firefox 121+). %>

[tasks.dev]
description = "Create/migrate DB if needed, then start the dev server"
run = """
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
bin/rails db:prepare
bin/dev
"""

[tasks."deploy:dev"]
description = "Run local development with live source mounting"
run = """
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# External bridge network shared with sandbox/WeTTY/Tailscale containers
if ! docker network inspect sandcastle-web >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Creating sandcastle-web network..."
  docker network create sandcastle-web
fi

# Detect Docker socket GID so the web/worker containers can talk to dockerd
if [ -z "${DOCKER_GID:-}" ]; then
  if [ "$(uname)" = "Darwin" ]; then
    DOCKER_GID=$(stat -f '%g' /var/run/docker.sock 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  else
    DOCKER_GID=$(stat -c '%g' /var/run/docker.sock 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  fi
  export DOCKER_GID
fi

# Default host for Traefik routing; override with DEV_HOST=foo.local
export DEV_HOST="${DEV_HOST:-localhost}"

# Pick a UID/GID for the in-container sandcastle user. Default to the host
# user's IDs so bind-mounted source files stay owned by you on disk. If the
# outer environment uses a restricted user namespace (e.g. Sysbox), the
# production default of 220568 would exceed the range and fail with EINVAL
# on chown/setgroups — the host UID is always within range.
if [ -z "${SANDCASTLE_UID:-}" ]; then
  if [ -r /proc/self/uid_map ]; then
    range=$(awk 'END{print $3}' /proc/self/uid_map)
    if [ -n "$range" ] && [ "$range" -lt 220569 ]; then
      echo "Detected restricted userns (range=$range); using host UID/GID for sandcastle user."
      SANDCASTLE_UID=$(id -u)
      SANDCASTLE_GID=$(id -g)
    fi
  fi
fi
export SANDCASTLE_UID="${SANDCASTLE_UID:-220568}"

services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.6
    container_name: sandcastle-traefik
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
      - "3000-3099:3000-3099"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/etc/traefik
      - ./.cert:/certs:ro
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic

<% content_for(:title) { "Getting Started — Sandcastle" } %>

Getting Started

Everything you need to go from zero to a running sandbox.

<% host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "sandcastle.example.com") %> <%# ── Install ── %>

1. Install the CLI

Download the latest release from <%= link_to "GitHub Releases", "https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/releases/latest", target: "_blank", class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 underline" %>, then extract it: 

tar xzf sandcastle_*.tar.gz
sudo mv sandcastle /usr/local/bin/
<%# ── Login ── %>

2. Log in

Point the CLI at this server and authenticate via your browser:

sandcastle login https://<%= host %>
<%# ── Tailscale ── %>

3. Enable Tailscale (recommended)

Each sandbox gets its own Tailscale IP so you can reach services directly — no port forwarding needed. 

# Interactive login — opens a browser URL to authenticate
sandcastle tailscale enable

# Or use an auth key for headless setups
sandcastle tailscale enable --auth-key tskey-auth-...

After authenticating, approve the advertised subnet routes in the Tailscale admin console.

<%# ── Create ── %>

4. Create a sandbox

Web UI — click the "Create Sandcastle" button on your dashboard to create a sandbox with all available options:

  • Name — unique identifier for your sandbox
  • Container Image — custom image or default
  • Snapshot — restore from an existing snapshot
  • Mount Home — persistent home directory across all sandboxes
  • Data Path — mount user data (or subpath) to /persisted
  • Tailscale — connect to your Tailscale network
  • Docker daemon — enable/disable Docker-in-Docker
  • Temporary — auto-remove when you disconnect

Or use the CLI for quick creation:

Quick throwaway sandbox — deleted when you disconnect:

sandcastle create scratch --rm

Persistent sandbox — keeps your home directory and data across restarts:

sandcastle create my-dev --home --data

Lightweight sandbox — no Docker daemon or VNC (faster startup):

sandcastle create lean --no-docker --no-vnc
Tip: Make these flags the default with config or env vars:
sandcastle config set mount_home true
sandcastle config set data_path .
sandcastle config set vnc false
sandcastle config set docker false
Or via env: export SANDCASTLE_HOME=1 SANDCASTLE_DATA=. SANDCASTLE_VNC=false
<%# ── Connect ── %>

content.content:
1	import { Controller } from "@hotwired/stimulus"
2	
3	// Switch visible panel based on the URL hash (#profile, #tokens, etc.).
4	// Every form inside the controlled element gets a hidden <input name="tab">
5	// whose value tracks the active tab, so the server can redirect back with
6	// the correct fragment.
7	export default class extends Controller {
8	  static targets = ["tab", "panel"]
9	  static values = { default: String }
10	
11	  connect() {
12	    this.ensureTabInputs()
13	    this.activate(this.currentTabId(), { updateHash: false })
14	    this.hashChanged = () => this.activate(this.currentTabId(), { updateHash: false })
15	    window.addEventListener("hashchange", this.hashChanged)
16	  }
17	
18	  disconnect() {
19	    window.removeEventListener("hashchange", this.hashChanged)
20	  }
21	
22	  select(event) {
23	    event.preventDefault()
24	    const id = event.currentTarget.dataset.tabId
25	    this.activate(id, { updateHash: true })
26	  }
27	
28	  // Preference order: URL hash (explicit), sessionStorage (last active in this
29	  // tab), Stimulus default, first panel. sessionStorage is the fallback for
30	  // Turbo Drive, which strips the redirect Location's fragment on form posts.
31	  currentTabId() {
32	    const valid = id => id && this.panelTargets.some(p => p.dataset.tabId === id)
33	    const hash = window.location.hash.replace(/^#/, "")
34	    if (valid(hash)) return hash
35	    const stored = sessionStorage.getItem(this.storageKey)
36	    if (valid(stored)) return stored
37	    return this.defaultValue || this.panelTargets[0]?.dataset.tabId
38	  }
39	
40	  get storageKey() { return "settings-active-tab" }
41	
42	  activate(id, { updateHash }) {
43	    if (!id) return
44	    this.panelTargets.forEach(panel => {
45	      panel.hidden = panel.dataset.tabId !== id
46	    })
47	    this.tabTargets.forEach(tab => {
48	      const active = tab.dataset.tabId === id
49	      tab.classList.toggle("border-blue-600", active)
50	      tab.classList.toggle("text-blue-600", active)
51	      tab.classList.toggle("border-transparent", !active)
52	      tab.classList.toggle("text-gray-500", !active)
53	      tab.setAttribute("aria-selected", active ? "true" : "false")
54	    })
55	    if (updateHash) {
56	      // replaceState so we don't pile up history entries for every tab click.
57	      history.replaceState(null, "", `#${id}`)
58	    }
59	    sessionStorage.setItem(this.storageKey, id)
60	    this.syncTabInputs(id)
61	  }
62	
63	  // Inject a hidden <input name="tab"> into every form once so it's serialized
64	  // with the request. We update the value on each tab switch.
65	  ensureTabInputs() {
66	    this.element.querySelectorAll("form").forEach(form => {
67	      if (form.querySelector('input[name="tab"][data-tab-injected]')) return
68	      const input = document.createElement("input")
69	      input.type = "hidden"
70	      input.name = "tab"
71	      input.dataset.tabInjected = "true"
72	      form.appendChild(input)
73	    })
74	  }
75	
76	  syncTabInputs(id) {
77	    this.element.querySelectorAll('input[name="tab"][data-tab-injected]').forEach(input => {
78	      input.value = id
79	    })
80	  }
81	}
82

<%# ── Sandbox Archive Section ── %>

Sandbox Archive

<% default_retention_days = Setting.instance.sandbox_archive_retention_days || 30 %>

When you remove a sandbox it is archived (soft-deleted) instead of immediately purged, so you can restore it if needed. Leave blank to use the system default (<%= default_retention_days %> days). Set to 0 to disable archiving (immediate purge on remove).

python3 -c "
import re, sys
for f in sys.argv[1:]:
    with open(f) as fh:
        src = fh.read()
    # Remove <% %> comments <%# ... %>
    src_ = re.sub(r'<%#.*?%>', '', src, flags=re.DOTALL)
    opens = 0
    closes = 0
    # Find all <% ... %> blocks
    for m in re.finditer(r'<%=?-?\s*(.*?)\s*-?%>', src_, flags=re.DOTALL):
        body = m.group(1)
        # opens: 'do', 'do |x|', 'if ', 'unless ', 'elsif' needs closure
        if re.search(r'\bdo\s*(\|[^|]*\|)?\s*$', body):
            opens += 1
        elif re.match(r'^\s*(if|unless)\b', body):
            opens += 1
        elif re.match(r'^\s*(def|module|class|begin|case|until|while|for)\b', body):
            opens += 1
        elif body.strip() == 'end':
            closes += 1
    print(f'{f}: opens={opens} closes={closes} diff={opens-closes}')

content.text:
Tested current vs. fixed layout in Chrome at iPhone (390w) and iPad (820w). The dev container couldn't build the Rails app (DNS blocked), so I built a Tailwind-CDN static preview that faithfully mirrors the real ERB structure and verified visually — no horizontal overflow at either size.

**Before at 390w:** navbar 653px (overflows), amber Tailscale banner fills the screen, dashboard button pushed offscreen, sandbox action buttons clipped.

**After:**
- **`shared/_navbar.html.erb`** — turned into `<details>/<summary>` (no JS). `<md` shows hamburger + drawer; `md+` keeps the desktop inline nav. Admin badge also visible next to brand on mobile. Drawer has solid `bg-white shadow-lg` so it doesn't bleed through the amber banner.
- **`layouts/application.html.erb`** — dev banner shows shortened text + hides icons on `<sm`; Tailscale warning stacks button below text on `<sm`; main padding becomes `pt-44 sm:pt-40` when warning is shown.
- **`dashboard/index.html.erb`** — header + create button stack on `<sm`, smaller heading.
- **`dashboard/_sandbox.html.erb`** — pills and action buttons each flex-wrap; CPU/MEM row drops below on `<sm`.
- **`sandboxes/show.html.erb`** — routes + snapshots tables wrapped in `overflow-x-auto`, route form stacks domain/port+submit, header "← Dashboard" shrinks to `←` on mobile.
- **`settings/show.html.erb`** — card padding `p-4 sm:p-6`; new-token form and custom-links rows stack on `<sm`.

[tasks."release"]
alias = "release:patch"
description = "Bump patch version, tag, push, and wait for CI to build the release"
run = """
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Check if HEAD already has a tag
if git describe --exact-match --tags HEAD 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "ERROR: Current commit already has a tag. Make new commits before releasing."
  exit 1
fi

# Get the latest tag
latest=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo "v0.0.0")
echo "Current version: $latest"

# Parse and bump patch
IFS='.' read -r major minor patch <<< "${latest#v}"
new_version="v${major}.${minor}.$((patch + 1))"
echo "New version:     $new_version"

# Tag and push
git tag "$new_version"
git push origin "$new_version"
echo ""
echo "Tag $new_version pushed. GitHub Actions will build the release."
echo "https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/actions"
"""

[tasks."release:minor"]
